purpose: Formally resolves the implementation decisions and open questions identified in docs/app/MVP_PRODUCT_SPEC.md, so the implementation phase has one binding reference instead of re-deciding these points ad hoc during coding. This record does not authorize building by itself — see PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json v1.1.0's applicationBuildAuthorization for the authorization this record relies on.

A_applicationMode.title: Application mode
statements: The first application supports only internalReviewer mode.
It is not a student production mode.
A persistent Draft / Review Required banner is mandatory and must remain visible on every route.

Blocked pilot content may be rendered only because the application is an internal review environment, viewed by authorized internal reviewers, instructors, and students in a supervised pilot context — not the general public.

Rendering content inside this application does not alter its governance status. No record's blockingStatus, studentFacingAllowed, or studentPublicationAuthorized changes as a result of being displayed.

B_visibilityHandling.title: Visibility handling
statements: In the primary learner-style topic flow, render only content blocks whose visibility permits learner presentation (visibility: "shared" or visibility: "student", per the source schema).
Preserve the original visibility metadata on every normalized record; the adapter must not strip or overwrite it.
Do not silently promote instructor-only material into the main learner flow.
Records with visibility: "instructor" are rendered only inside a separate, collapsed internal-review panel, not in the main learner-style flow.
That panel is labeled clearly, bilingually: "Instructor Review Note" / "ملاحظة لمراجعة المدرّس".
Instructor-visibility records are never presented as ordinary student-facing content, in either language.

Their source record IDs remain visible in development or reviewer metadata (e.g. a dev-mode inspector or reviewer-only annotation), so internal reviewers can trace exactly which record they are looking at.

This decision specifically applies to the misconception content blocks in all four topics, which are uniformly visibility: "instructor" in the source schema (ch01-t02-block-misconception, ch01-t03-block-misconception, ch01-t08-block-misconception, ch01-t10-block-misconception) — each renders inside the collapsed Instructor Review Note panel, not as a normal page section.

C_learningObjective.title: Learning objective
statements: No dedicated learner-visible learning-objective content block currently exists in the source schema — it exists only inside instructorScript.learningObjectives[], which has no visibility field distinguishing it from clearly instructor-only fields on the same record.
Do not synthesize, rewrite, or infer a learning objective from other records.

instructorScript may supply learning-objective information only within internal reviewer metadata (e.g. the same collapsed internal-review panel used for instructor-visibility content, or an equivalent reviewer-only surface) — never as an ordinary learner-facing section, because no explicit learner-visible field authorizes that use.
Omit the learning-objective section entirely from the learner-style page when no explicitly authorized learner-visible field exists for it.
This omission is preferable to inventing content, and is not treated as a defect to silently work around.

D_keyConceptAndSummary.title: Key concept and summary
statements: Do not synthesize a key concept or summary section from unrelated records.
Display a summary section only when the adapter finds an explicit, semantically appropriate source record for it.

None of the four topics' current schemas contain a record explicitly designated as a summary/key-concept block distinct from the mainIdea contentBlock already used earlier in the page (per MVP_PRODUCT_SPEC.md §5, position 3); absent a new explicit field, the summary section is omitted rather than re-deriving or duplicating the mainIdea text under a different heading.
Otherwise, omit the section cleanly.
The page layout must support optional sections without leaving empty headings, empty containers, or irregular spacing where an omitted section would have been.

E_equations.title: Equations
statements: For the first implementation, treat the existing authored equation strings (from each topic's equationSet contentBlock) as source content, rendered as-is, not rewritten or re-derived.
Render equations inside explicit LTR containers, isolated from surrounding Arabic prose, matching the pattern already proven in the four pilot SVGs.
Preserve Unicode superscripts and subscripts already present in the source text.

Preserve italic-variable and upright-unit styling where the source representation supports distinguishing them (e.g. via semantic markup applied during rendering, consistent with MVP_PRODUCT_SPEC.md §9).
Do not rewrite equation content.
Do not introduce a general equation parser in the initial implementation.

Do not add KaTeX or MathJax unless an implementation spike demonstrates that semantic HTML and controlled typography cannot render the actual pilot equations (a_c = v^2/r; a = -g / a = +g; f = 1/T, T = 1/f; the length/area/volume k, k^2, k^3 relationships) accurately.

Equation-library adoption (KaTeX, MathJax, or equivalent) is documented as an implementation escalation decision to be made and recorded if and when the spike above demonstrates the need — it is not a default dependency of this MVP, and is listed under unresolvedItems below rather than pre-approved here.

F_contentSourceAndAdapter.title: Content source and adapter
statements: The four pilot JSON files remain the source of truth for all instructional content.
React components must not contain copied instructional content; components receive normalized data as props only.
The adapter must be read-only against the content and asset files.
The adapter must preserve recordId, topicId, blockType, visibility, source/provenance lineage, blocking state, and both English and Arabic localization data for every record it normalizes.
Unsupported or malformed records must produce internal development diagnostics (console/dev-overlay), not silent omission or a broken render.
The adapter must not mutate, repair, or write back to any content file under any circumstance.
No missing field may be silently invented or defaulted to placeholder instructional text; a missing field is surfaced as a missing-content state (per MVP_PRODUCT_SPEC.md §16), not filled in.

G_bilingualBehavior.title: Bilingual behavior
statements: One language is visible at a time.
English uses LTR layout.
Arabic uses RTL layout.
Equations and SI unit symbols remain LTR even inside Arabic-mode pages.
Runtime machine translation is prohibited; Arabic content is only ever the authored ar fields already present in the source JSON.
Side-by-side bilingual mode is deferred, not built or stubbed in this MVP.
Arabic shaping (cursive glyph joining, correct RTL order) must be tested in the actual supported browser environment during implementation, not assumed from source-code correctness alone.

H_visualBehavior.title: Visual behavior
statements: Use the four existing SVG files (ch01-t02/t03/t08/t10-visual-001.svg) as read-only assets, embedded as authored.
Preserve each SVG's internal direction metadata and accessibility metadata (role="img", <title>, <desc>) exactly as authored.
Keep each SVG's internal amber Draft/Review-Required footer band visible; the application does not crop, mask, or hide it.
Use a zoomable or fullscreen viewer for narrow screens rather than shrinking the SVG inline to an illegible size.
Do not create mobile-specific SVG redraws during the initial MVP.

An SVG being available (assetStatus/availabilityStatus present, asset renders) does not mean it is finally approved — all four visuals remain reviewer: null / reviewedAt: null in their validation records, and the application's chrome must not imply otherwise.

I_applicationLocation.title: Application location
statements: Use apps/chapter1-mvp/ as the application root.
Keep all application source, package, configuration, test, and generated dependency-lock files inside this directory where technically practical.

Do not create application files at the repository root unless a later implementation task demonstrates a concrete technical requirement and receives explicit authorization for that specific exception.

J_statePersistence.title: State and persistence
statements: Initial MVP state is limited to exactly: current route, active language, open/closed solution-reveal state, open/closed visual-viewer state, and open/closed internal-reviewer-notes state.
Use local React state and URL routing for all of the above.
Do not include login, database persistence, cloud state, student progress, grades, or analytics of any kind in this MVP.

AMENDED by amendments[0] (ch01-mvp-implementation-decisions-001-amendment-001, 2026-07-19): the statements above remain the default for every topic and every piece of state not explicitly listed in that amendment. See amendments[0] for the one narrow, additional exception it authorizes.

K_gitState.title: Git state
statements: The current docs tree is untracked in Git as of this decision record's date.
No staging, commit, or push is authorized by this decision record.

Repository protection through Git (tracking the untracked docs tree, establishing a commit baseline) requires a separate, explicit project-owner authorization, not implied by this record or by PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json v1.1.0.
The implementation must not silently perform any version-control operation (no automatic git init, add, commit, or push as a side effect of scaffolding, building, or testing).

L_publicationState.title: Publication state
statements: internalMvpBuildAuthorized: true
localTestingAuthorized: true
publicDeploymentAuthorized: false
studentFacingAllowed: false
studentPublicationAuthorized: false
These five values are also recorded in machine-readable form in the top-level publicationState field of this document, which is authoritative for tooling; this list restates them for readability.

amendments.title: Scoped localStorage persistence for language and ch01-t01's interactive-presentation UI state
amendments.requestContext: Approved as part of the ch01-t01 (Fundamental Quantities) interactive-presentation feature request implemented in apps/chapter1-mvp/ PR #5 (branch feature/fundamental-quantities-interactions).
statements: In addition to decisions.J_statePersistence's original session-only state, the following two, and only these two, pieces of UI state may also be persisted to the browser's own localStorage, read and written client-side only: (1) the active language, app-wide; and (2), for ch01-t01 (Fundamental Quantities) only, its collapsible-section open/closed state and its review-card answer-reveal state.

Every other topic's solution-reveal, visual-viewer, and internal-reviewer-notes state remains exactly as decisions.J_statePersistence originally specified: local React state only, reset each session, no persistence.
This persistence is local-browser-storage only (the browser's own localStorage API): no user account, no authentication, no server-side or cloud synchronization, and no data ever leaves the device.

No student progress, grades, scores, correctness records, or analytics of any kind are persisted or transmitted by this amendment — only UI display preferences (which language is selected, which sections are currently expanded, whether an answer is currently shown).

This amendment does not change, and has no bearing on, studentFacingAllowed, studentPublicationAuthorized, publicDeploymentAuthorized, or any other governance/publication flag recorded in decisions.L_publicationState, this document's own publicationState field, or PILOT_AUTHORIZATION.json.
This amendment does not authorize login, a database, cloud state, or any analytics tooling of any kind in this MVP — decisions.J_statePersistence's prohibition on those remains fully in force.
Implementation reference: apps/chapter1-mvp/src/app/persistedState.ts (the single, guarded read/write helper used for every persisted value this amendment covers).

unresolvedItems.item: Whether a math-rendering library (KaTeX, MathJax, or equivalent) becomes necessary
unresolvedItems.resolutionPath: To be decided only after the implementation spike described in decisions.E_equations demonstrates that semantic HTML and controlled typography cannot accurately render the actual pilot equations. Record the outcome as an amendment to this decision record if escalation is needed.

unresolvedItems.item: Which exact automated accessibility tooling will be installed (e.g. axe-core, eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y, or equivalent)
unresolvedItems.resolutionPath: To be selected during Phase 6 of MVP_PRODUCT_SPEC.md's implementation sequence, subject to the same dependency-justification principle as all other tooling (installed only if it solves a demonstrated need).

unresolvedItems.item: Whether repository staging and commit of the docs tree (and later the application code) will be authorized
unresolvedItems.resolutionPath: Requires a separate, explicit project-owner authorization, per decisions.K_gitState. Not decided by this record.

unresolvedItems.item: What exact conditions will define final student-publication approval
unresolvedItems.resolutionPath: Governed by future, separate project-owner authorization changing studentFacingAllowed / studentPublicationAuthorized to true, following the same explicit governance process used for every other authorization in this repository. MVP_PRODUCT_SPEC.md §22 lists the conditions that must be met before that decision can even be considered; this record does not itself set or predict that decision.
